Complete Exclusion: Those Without Any Learning, Observance, or Character Must Be Avoided

micro_segments.title: Why Mention Both Personal and Through Agent? To Teach That Personal Performance is Preferable
The General Principle: Engaging One's Body in the Mitzvah Produces Greater Impact and Connection

macro_segments.title: The Requirement to See the Woman Before Engagement: Avoiding Mutual Repulsion

micro_segments.title: Rav Judah in the Name of Rav: Forbidden to Betroth a Woman Without Seeing Her First
The Reason: Lest He Find Something Repulsive and Violate the Obligation of Loving One's Fellow
Context of Marriage: Not Romantic Love, but Avoiding Mutual Contempt in a Practical Institution

micro_segments.title: For Women: Engaging Through Agent is Permitted (Not Prohibited)
The Principle of Reish Lakish: Better to Sit in Tandem Than to Sit Alone—Women's Pragmatism

micro_segments.title: The Question: Why Use the Language of Mitzvah for an Economic Transaction?

The Meaning of Two Stages: Commitment Preceding Lived Reality as Resilience Against Marital Hardship

macro_segments.title: Prohibition on Betrothing a Minor Daughter: The Father Cannot Override Her Consent

micro_segments.title: Rav Judah in the Name of Rav: Forbidden to Betroth One's Daughter When She is a Minor
The Reason: She Must Grow and Consent to Marry This Specific Person
Tosafot's Distinction: Adult Woman Agreed to Unknown Agent, but Minor Never Consented to Such Arrangement
Tosafot's Lament: In Our Time We Do Betroth Minors Due to Exile and Insecurity, to Protect Her Future

macro_segments.title: The Source and Scope of Agency (Shlichut): Learning from Divorce, Kiddushin, Terumah, and Korban Pesach

micro_segments.title: Where Do We Learn That Agency Exists? The Verse About Sending Away a Divorced Woman
Grammatical Interpretation: Both the Man and Woman Can Create an Agent; An Agent Can Create Another Agent
Learning Agency for Terumah: The Verse 'Also You' Includes Representatives in Separating Heave-Offering
Why Not Learn Agency for Kiddushin from Divorce? Divorce is One-Sided and Passive; Kiddushin is Two-Sided

macro_segments.title: Agency for Korban Pesach: The Group's Agent Slaughtering the Offering

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: A Group's Lamb Runs Away; They Send One Man to Find and Slaughter It
Learning Agency for Kodashim from Rabbi Yehoshua ben Korcha: All Must Slaughter, Yet One Person Does

macro_segments.title: Why Each Case of Agency Cannot Be Learned from the Others: Set Theory and Shared Characteristics

micro_segments.title: Kodashim Has: Kedusha, Machshava (Thought), and Maaseihem al Yedei Shaliach
Terumah Has: Kedusha and Machshava, but Not the Usual Delegation of Actions
Girushin Has: Only Chol (Mundane), No Kedusha or Machshava
The Gemara's Conclusion: Only Girushin and Kodashim Share No Overlap, So Agency Applies Universally
